# synesis

> AI research, engineering, and the occasional philosophical aside. Personal blog by Benjamin Han.

Site: https://benjaminhan.net/
Atom feed: https://benjaminhan.net/index.xml
Sitemap: https://benjaminhan.net/sitemap.xml

Each post is also available as Pandoc-flavored Markdown source at
`/posts/<slug>/index.qmd` (e.g. https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260425-reinforcing-abstract-thinking-in-reasoning-models/index.qmd).

## Posts

- [AbstRaL: Augmenting LLMs' Reasoning by Reinforcing Abstract Thinking](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260425-reinforcing-abstract-thinking-in-reasoning-models/): AbstRaL teaches LLMs to abstract a math word problem before solving it via symbolic tools, using reinforcement learning rewards on both abstraction faithfulness and answer correctness.
- [World History](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260425-world-history-scifi/): A speculative timeline from 2050 to 2150: augmented reality, Esosense, the collapse of MERIDIAN, and a Privacy Rights Management system built into human biology.
- [How to Be Anti-Social](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260424-anti-social/): Nate's satirical playbook for the patterns of thought that shrink conversations and deepen isolation.
- [The Global Edtech Boom Is Fading as Investors Look Elsewhere](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260424-edu-startups-decline/): Rest of World on K-12 edtech's funding collapse, with a closer look at the nonprofit-plus-frontier-API pattern that displaced venture-backed tutoring and what the decline says about AI's fit with K-12.
- [Elliott Bay Trail Run](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260424-elliott-bay-trail-run/): Two photos from today's run along Seattle's Elliott Bay Trail.
- [How LLMs Actually Work: A Visual Deep Dive](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260424-llm-illustration/): An interactive scrollable walkthrough of how large language models are built, from raw web text to a conversational assistant, based on Andrej Karpathy's deep dive.
- [Convergent Evolution: How Different Language Models Learn Similar Number Representations](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260424-llms-learn-numbers-similarly/): An empirical study showing that diverse language model architectures all develop periodic number embeddings, but only some go on to encode mod-T classes as linearly separable structure.
- [Anthropic Economic Index](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260423-anthropic-economic-index/): Anthropic's fourth Economic Index report introduces five economic primitives and uses them to revisit task speedup, deskilling, and aggregate productivity estimates.
- [Sequential KV Cache Compression via Probabilistic Language Tries](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260422-kv-cache-compression/): A two-layer scheme — prefix deduplication plus predictive delta coding — that compresses the KV cache as a sequence rather than per-vector, with a theoretical compression ratio of ~914,000× over TurboQuant at the Shannon limit.
- [Overzealous Coding Models](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260422-overzealous-coding-models/): Coding LLMs over-edit even when asked to fix a single off-by-one — and the problem is measurable, prompt-steerable, and LoRA trainable.
- [Proof Generation vs Digestion](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260422-proof-generation-vs-digestion/): Terence Tao decomposes mathematical problem-solving into generation, verification, and digestion — and warns that AI is racing through the first two while leaving the third behind.
- [Civilization's Three Stages, Applied to Mathematics](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260421-civilizations-three-stages/): Terence Tao on Mastodon, applying Douglas Adams' Survival/Inquiry/Sophistication phases to mathematics — and where AI is pushing the field next.
- [Prompt Repetition Improves Non-Reasoning LLMs](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260421-prompt-repetition/): Leviathan, Kalman, and Matias find that simply repeating the input prompt boosts performance on Gemini, GPT, Claude, and Deepseek — no extra tokens, no extra latency.
- [Claude Opus 4.7's New Tokenizer: ~40% More Tokens Than 4.6](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260420-opus-47-token-count/): Simon Willison's Claude Token Counter shows Opus 4.7's new tokenizer maps the same input to ~1.46× more tokens than 4.6, raising effective cost ~40% at unchanged per-token pricing.
- [A Rube Goldberg Pipeline for Sharing Links](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260420-rube-goldberg-publishing/): How a single iOS share-sheet tap traverses half a dozen systems (Pythonista, GitHub, Quarto, Mastodon) to land a blog post and a toot.
- [Sounds of Joy](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260420-sounds-of-joy/): An interactive sound-mapped collection of 36 mechanical keyboards — click any one and type to hear it.
- [Earth Day 10K with My Daughter — First in Our Age Groups](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260418-earth-day-10k-with-daughter/): An Earth Day team race with my daughter — both #1 in our age groups, even after three days off and a near-sleepless night.
- [Silver Bars and Language Models: Reading *Babel* in the Age of LLMs](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260417-babel-llm-comparison/): R.F. Kuang finished describing a world in which linguistic knowledge is industrialized and centralized just as our world began building exactly that.
- [APIs, Tools, Skills, and Where MCP Fits](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260414-api-mcp-tool-skill/): APIs, tools, and skills form a rough abstraction ladder. MCP is a different kind of thing: a protocol that crosscuts the ladder.
- [Chain-of-Thought, by Way of 4chan](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260414-reasoning-origin-link/): Chain-of-thought may have roots in 4chan. The Atlantic traces the genealogy.
- ["Verified" Doesn't Mean "Nothing Can Go Wrong"](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260413-verified-not-safe/): A Claude agent found a buffer overflow in the Lean runtime — under code that was itself formally verified.
- [CoachGPT, in Practice](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260412-coachgpt-marathon/): Bloomberg trained for the Paris marathon with ChatGPT. The model's own closing line: the future is people who learn to build disciplined working relationships with AI.
- [Why LLMs Still Stumble Over Time](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260411-llm-temporal-reasoning/): Temporal reasoning in LLMs isn't one skill but a bundle of them — and even when the calendar math is right, it stays brittle.
- [iPhone, Artemis II, Moon](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260406-iphone-on-artemis/): Astronauts onboard Artemis II took pictures with the iPhone 17 Pro Max.
- [Filesystems vs. RAG](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260404-filesystem-vs-rag/): Replacing a docs RAG flow with a virtual filesystem reframes the question from "what's relevant?" to "how does the model investigate?"
- [Artemis II Launches on Apple's 50th Anniversary](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260402-artemis-launch/): The first crewed lunar journey since Apollo 17 in 1972 — coincident, by accident, with Apple turning fifty.
- [The Revenge of the Data Scientist](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260402-hamel-revenge-data-scientist/): Hamel Husain on the surrounding machinery — logs, metrics, traces, tests, specs — that turns model calls into a working agentic system.
- [Learning to Reason in 13 Parameters](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260331-tinylora/): TinyLoRA: an 8B Qwen2.5 reaches 91% on GSM8K with only 13 trained bf16 parameters — 26 bytes of learned weights.
- [How Apple Became Apple](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260330-apple-oral-history/): Fast Company's oral history of Apple's earliest days, told by the people who lived it — published as Apple turns 50.
- [Redmond City Marathon: #30, with a Sprained Ankle](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260329-redmond-city-marathon/): Marathon #30 at the Redmond City Marathon — first race paced by HR. A sprained ankle at mile 2.6 didn't stop the 7-week streak.
- [LLM Neuroanatomy: Topping the Leaderboard Without Changing a Weight](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260324-llm-neuroanatomy/): Duplicate a block of middle transformer layers and run them twice at inference. The power of thinking it over twice.
- [Language Puzzles, NACLO, and a Note of Thanks](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260319-language-puzzles/): Scientific American's piece on the North American Computational Linguistics Open Competition — and a personal thank-you to the people who built the world that drew me into linguistics.
- [Snowflake Cortex AI Escapes Sandbox and Executes Malware](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260319-snowflake-cortex-sandbox-escape/): An indirect prompt injection that turned a repo README into shell commands run outside the sandbox — and the multi-layer observability gap it exposed.
- [Journey into Coding with AI [3/4]: Decision-Bound Programming](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260315-ai-coding-decision-bound/): AI accelerates code generation but moves the bottleneck to interpretation, comparison, and judgment. The next generation of programming tools should be decision support systems.
- [Knuth's Hamiltonian Cycles, Solved by Claude](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260308-knuth-hamiltonian-claude/): Donald Knuth's Hamiltonian cycle decomposition problem from The Art of Computer Programming was solved by Claude Opus 4.6.
- [Woodinville Half: HM #76, the Comeback](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260308-woodinville-half-marathon/): 1:44:20 at the Woodinville Half. Finally feeling back after a rough January — VO₂max and easy pace both recovered.
- [iPhone and iPad Approved for Classified NATO Information](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260226-iphone-ipad-nato-classified/): The first and only consumer devices to meet these international government security standards.
- [Marcus vs. Brundage: A Concrete AGI Bet](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260223-marcus-brundage-agi-bet/): What do we actually mean by AGI? Marcus and Brundage put $22,000 on a 10-task definition by end of 2027 — and prediction markets disagree sharply with Brundage.
- [The 'CS Exodus' as Discipline Evolution](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260215-cs-exodus-ai-native-computing/): What looks like an exodus from Computer Science is more accurately a shift toward AI-native computing — the latest move in a long lineage that began with Electrical Engineering.
- [Anthropic on AI Coding: Scaffold, Not Substitute](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260130-ai-coding-scaffold-not-substitute/): Anthropic's study finds that delegating to AI hurts comprehension, but using it for conceptual questions and error explanations correlates with better learning.
- [Humans Need Rest, Even When Machines Don't](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260119-burning-out-with-ai-coding-agents/): Ars Technica on burning out with AI coding agents — and the suggestion that knowledge workers may need new protections.
- [Inside the M5: As Big as New Jersey](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260119-m5-chip-new-jersey/): Think of the state of New Jersey next time you whip out your iPhone.
- [Bridle Trails 50K: Hot Cocoa, Sprained Ankle, No Horseshit](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20260112-bridle-trails-50k/): My 50K race went sideways at loop 4 with an ankle/calf injury, but I held the rule that matters in trail running.
- [End of 2025: 2,543 Miles, 342 Days, Six PRs](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251231-end-of-2025-running-retrospective/): End-of-year retrospective: 2,543.3 miles in 2025 across 342 days; PRs at every distance from 5K to 50K.
- [Five Takes from Zhengdong Wang's 2025 Letter](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251231-zhengdong-wang-2025-letter/): A thoughtful annual letter on where AI actually is, and where it's heading.
- [Parkrun Turkey Trot: 22:09 on Thanksgiving](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251127-parkrun-turkey-trot/): A record 270+ runners showed up; not a PR but felt strong the whole way.
- ["Skills" Are Not Software Engineering](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251124-skills-not-software-engineering/): Anthropic's skills framework is a meaningful step for agent tooling — but skills cannot replace the orchestration, contracts, and correctness guarantees that real software systems need. A hybrid model is what's next.
- [9th 50K Long Run: Redmond Library to the Seattle Office](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251109-redmond-to-seattle-50k-long-run/): 31.12 miles, two McDonald's pit stops, ended at the office with a hot shower.
- [LLMs Can Get Brain Rot Too](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251022-llm-brain-rot/): Training on viral, low-quality data costs models reasoning ability and long-context understanding — and clean retraining doesn't fully undo it.
- [Karpathy: AGI Is Still a Decade Away](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251018-karpathy-agi-decade-away/): Four takeaways from Karpathy's chat with Dwarkesh — the decade of agents, RL through a straw, and why coding LLMs still aren't reliable collaborators.
- [Snohomish River Run: Marathon #29, Survived from Mile 18](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251013-snohomish-marathon/): 4th marathon of the year (29th overall): chip time 3:38:09, perfect weather — until gravel and a calf flare-up at mile 18.
- [Vibe Engineering](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251008-vibe-engineering/): Vibe engineering = using AI and coding agents responsibly, as an acceleration tool, while retaining accountability, oversight, and engineering discipline.
- [Terence Tao's ChatGPT Research Buddy](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20251002-terence-tao-chatgpt-research-buddy/): Terence Tao showed how he used ChatGPT like a research buddy — back-and-forth on a MathOverflow counterexample, hours saved, even a few math slips caught.
- [A Pure Republic of Classical Music](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250912-apple-music-classical-editors/): On Apple Music Classical's anonymous editors — and why their anonymity is exactly what lets them recommend purely on the merits.
- [Journey into Coding with AI [2/4]: Shifting Gears](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250906-coding-with-ai-2-shifting-gears/): From stick shift to starship console: how much control do we cede to coding AI, and what role remains for humans?
- [Journey into Coding with AI [1/4]: Running Back to Code](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250905-coding-with-ai-1-running-back-to-code/): A month in with coding AI: it's like assembling a small team of servant interns. The next abstraction step in programming — concepts compiled into code.
- [Embedding Limits: A Linear-Algebra Note (and Kernel Tricks)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250903-embedding-limits-kernel-tricks/): A follow-up callout: the bound rests on rank(AB) ≤ min(rank(A), rank(B)) — a property of dot-product scoring, not embeddings per se. Plus one more way out: kernel tricks.
- [Redmond Harvest Half: HM #64, 1:40:45](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250901-redmond-harvest-half/): 1:40:45 at the Redmond Harvest Half — 64th HM since 2022; CoachGPT wasn't too happy.
- [Embeddings Hit a Theoretical Ceiling](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250831-embedding-retrieval-limits/): A new paper proves that single-vector retrieval has a hard dimension-dependent capacity bound — and shows three ways out: cross-encoders, multi-vector, sparse.
- [State of AI in Business 2025: Why 95% Get Zero P&L](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250830-state-of-ai-in-business-2025/): $30–40B invested, 95% of orgs see no P&L impact — five traits the winning systems share.
- [How Apple AirPods Work](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250828-how-airpods-work/): So much wonderful tech and engineering goes into this teeny tiny daily driver.
- [Tunnel Vision Marathon: Heat, Cramps, and a 25 sec/mile Lesson](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250811-tunnel-vision-marathon/): 3:40:01 at Tunnel Vision — far from BQ. Then a runner's-world piece on heat impact reframed everything.
- [Apple Shadyside, Old and New](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250808-apple-shadyside-store/): I still remember September 4, 2004 — standing in line for the opening of the Apple Shadyside store while at CMU. Goodbye, old store. Welcome, new one.
- [Mid-Year Running Recap: PRs at Every Distance](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250803-mid-year-running-recap/): 1,697 mi YTD, 283 ahead of plan. PRs at every distance from 5K to half marathon. 11 sec from sub-21 5K, 5 min from BQ.
- [MAST: A Failure Taxonomy for Multi-Agent Systems](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250802-mast-multi-agent-failures/): First empirically grounded taxonomy of why MAS fail — 14 modes across specification, alignment, and verification — and small interventions that move the needle 9–16%.
- [MSR: Which Occupations GenAI Is Actually Used In](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250801-msr-genai-occupations/): MSR's analysis of real-world GenAI usage by occupation — knowledge work and information-providing roles top the list.
- [How Anthropic Teams Use Claude Code](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250725-anthropic-claude-code-teams/): Two patterns from Anthropic's internal teams: treat Claude like a slot machine (DS/ML) or an iterative partner (Product Eng).
- [Chollet: How We Get to AGI](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250707-chollet-how-we-get-to-agi/): Notes from François Chollet's talk: intelligence as process, the ARC suite, and why we need to combine type-1 (continuous) and type-2 (discrete) abstractions.
- ['Positive Review Only': Hidden AI Prompts in Papers](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250705-positive-review-only/): Researchers hiding AI prompts in academic papers to manipulate peer review — a Nikkei Asia investigation. Please don't do this.
- [Apple Music Turns 10](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250630-apple-music-10-years/): 10 years ago today, Apple Music went live.
- [20 Years of Podcasts on iTunes](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250628-apple-podcasting-mainstream/): 20 years ago today, podcasts went mainstream.
- [For Alfred Brendel](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250619-beethoven-fur-elise-brendel/): On the passing of Alfred Brendel — and one Beethoven Bagatelle that has stayed with me.
- [Karpathy: Software Is Changing (Again)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250619-karpathy-software-changing-again/): Notes from Karpathy's talk: Software 3.0, LLMs as fallible people spirits, the autonomy slider, and the decade of agents.
- [ICR² Accepted to ACL 2025 Findings](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250610-icr2-acl-acceptance/): Our paper on Eliciting In-context Retrieval and Reasoning for Long-context LLMs has been accepted into ACL 2025 Findings.
- [Mill Town Marathon: 3:24:01, Closer to BQ](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250413-mill-town-marathon/): Marathon PR by ~6 min; 27 minutes off last year's time on the same course.
- [World's Fastest 10K + Three PRs in Two Weeks](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250323-worlds-fastest-10k-and-multiple-prs/): 10K PR (43:56), 5K PR (21:23), and a half marathon PR (1:39:07) — all within two weeks.
- [Apple Music Classical on the Web](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250313-apple-music-classical-web/): Classical music lovers, rejoice — Apple Music Classical is now available on the web.
- [Rancho San Antonio Trail Run, Cupertino](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250302-rancho-san-antonio-trail-run/): 9.67 mi, 1,545 ft EG on Rancho San Antonio trails — 3 hours of daylight, well spent.
- [DeepSeek vs ChatGPT on Ethical Questions](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250208-deepseek-chatgpt-ethics/): ChatGPT seems to follow deontological ethics (rules-based) while DeepSeek aligns with consequentialism (outcome-based). Pre-training or RL-tuning?
- [Eliciting In-context Retrieval and Reasoning for Long-context LLMs (Preprint)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250115-icr2-preprint-announcement/): Our fresh preprint on ICR²: if LLMs had context windows large enough for a whole knowledge base, RAG could collapse into a single step — but are current LCLMs up to the task?
- [Bridle Trails Winter 50K: My First Ultra](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20250112-bridle-trails-50k/): 5:05:42 at the Bridle Trails Winter Running Festival — 9th of 45 runners, in the dark, with a fall on a smooth rock and a knee that nearly ended the race.
- [End of 2024: 2,375 Miles, 18 Marathons, 4 50Ks](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20241231-end-of-2024-running-retrospective/): End-of-year retrospective: 2,375.4 miles in 2024, 18 full marathons (5 races) and 4 50K runs, with PRs from 5K to 50K and a 34.5-mile max.
- [Sutskever at NeurIPS 2024: Pre-Training Era Is Over](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20241213-sutskever-pretraining-over/): Ilya Sutskever's Test of Time award talk at NeurIPS 2024 — pre-training is over, next up is agents/synthetic data/inference-time compute, and reasoning is unpredictable.
- [Seattle Marathon 2024: 18th Marathon, GAP PR](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20241201-seattle-marathon-2024/): Official 3:33:04 at Seattle Marathon 2024 — 1:49 short of PR, but a hillier course, GAP-faster than Snohomish, and top 9% in age group.
- [Languages of New York City](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20241127-nyc-languages-map/): ~700 language varieties from all over the world used in NYC — about 10% of world languages.
- [Parkrun PR + 34-Mile Ultra](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20241116-parkrun-pr-34mi-ultra/): Parkrun 5K PR (21:09 official) — 7th PR of the year. Plus my longest run yet: a 34-mile ultramarathon on the Snoqualmie Valley Trail.
- [20 Seconds of Thinking, 100,000× More Data](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20241114-noam-brown-test-time-compute/): Noam Brown at TED AI: a poker bot thinking for 20 seconds matched scaling the model 100,000× and training it 100,000× longer.
- [How Well Can Transformers Build World Models?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20241108-transformers-world-models/): Two negative results: SOTA models drop on GSM8K when names/numbers change, and DFA-based metrics show big gaps when the world shifts.
- [Snohomish River Run 2024: Marathon PR by 7 Minutes](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20241013-snohomish-marathon-pr/): 3:31:15 at Snohomish River Run — a 7-minute marathon PR. Slower slow runs, no skipped intervals, better fueling, and a 180-bpm playlist saved the day at mile 14.
- [6th Parkrun PR + 2024 Stretch Goals Done](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20241005-parkrun-strava-pr-2024-goals/): 21:16 at Parkrun 5K — 6th PR of the year. All three 2024 stretch goals checked: sub-7' pace, 6 PRs, 3 marathon races.
- [Around Lake Sammamish: A 27.71-Mile Run](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240922-lake-sammamish-27mi-loop/): 13th marathon-length run of 2024, looping Lake Sammamish — a planned 31-mile road run that turned into trail running when Weowna Park happened at mile 12.
- [Crossing Lake Washington: A 33.01-Mile Run](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240915-lake-washington-33mi-crossing/): Second 50K+ run of 2024 and the longest yet — 33.01 miles, 5:21:26 moving time, looping Lake Washington across multiple trails.
- [Redmond Harvest Half 2024: 1:45:24 After Flu Recovery](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240902-redmond-harvest-half-2024/): 1:45:24 at Redmond Harvest Half Marathon — 12 minutes faster than last year, after recovering from August's PR-making marathon and a flu just days earlier.
- [Tunnel Vision Marathon 2024: Bathroom, Bad Shoes, OOD](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240811-tunnel-vision-marathon-2024/): 3:46:13 at Tunnel Vision Marathon — PRs at 10K/15K/HM splits, but a bathroom emergency, slightly large shoes, and untrained quads on a downhill course did the rest.
- [Time-Sensitive Knowledge Editing via Efficient Fine-Tuning (ACL 2024)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240809-acl2024-knowledge-editing/): PEFT outperforms locate-and-edit for knowledge editing — and applying LoRA to MLP and attention parameters stays robust as the number of edits grows.
- [30th Half Marathon: Iron Horse Trail BQ Prep](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240728-iron-horse-half-marathon-30th/): 30th HM on the Iron Horse Trail — 9 miles up 1,287ft and 8 miles down. Down-hill pace at 7'40" makes BQ feel within reach for the August marathon.
- [Tunnel Marathon Course Recon: 53 Miles Over Two Sundays](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240714-tunnel-marathon-course-recon/): 10th marathon since January, run as a recon of the August Tunnel Marathon course — 26.69 miles one weekend, 26.39 the next, both halves of the course in opposite directions.
- [First 50K: Carnation to North Bend and Back](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240630-50k-carnation-north-bend/): Longest run yet — 50K/31 miles from Carnation to North Bend and back, 5:18:05 moving time. Plenty of stops for photos and ice cream.
- [10K for 7 Days at NAACL 2024 (Mexico City)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240622-naacl2024-running/): We ran 10K every morning for a week at 2,240 m elevation alongside the conference.
- [TWEAK at NAACL 2024: Decoding Without Hallucinations](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240615-tweak-naacl-2024/): TWEAK ranks decoding candidates by how well their continuations support the input facts — a decoding-only fix that improves faithfulness with minimal quality loss.
- [Are Researchers Using LLMs to Write Their Papers?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240603-llm-usage-scientific-papers/): Liang et al. analyze 950k papers and find up to 17.5% show signs of LLM usage in Computer Science.
- [Drumheller Marathon 2024: 220 Laps to a New PR](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240601-drumheller-marathon-2024/): First-ever marathon at the UW Drumheller race — 220 laps, 3:38:57 chip time (PR), with a left ankle scare at mile 20 saved by switching to cushioned shoes.
- [AI on Trial: Legal Models Hallucinate in 1 out of 6 Queries](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240531-legal-ai-hallucinations/): RAG-based legal AI tools from LexisNexis and Thomson Reuters each hallucinate more than 17% of the time, despite claims of being hallucination-free.
- [May the Fourth at Parkrun](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240504-parkrun-volunteer-may-the-fourth/): Volunteering at Parkrun on Star Wars day.
- [Portugal Trip: Three Runs and a SINFO Talk](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240415-portugal-runs-sinfo-talk/): Visiting Portugal for SINFO — Porto, Aveiro, Coimbra, Sintra, Cascais, Lisbon — with three runs (Porto two bridges, Sintra to Castelo dos Mouros, Lisbon).
- [Two K2T Papers Accepted: TWEAK at NAACL, LAGRANGE at LREC-COLING (2024)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240404-tweak-lagrange-acceptance/): Two recently published papers on knowledge-to-text generation: TWEAK (decoding-time hallucination reduction) and LAGRANGE (cyclic-evaluation-built K2T dataset).
- [Easter Sunday on the Snoqualmie Valley Trail](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240331-snoqualmie-valley-trail-easter/): 21.78 miles southbound from Duvall and back. No PR setting — just taking Bus 11 (my legs) on a scenic ride.
- [Generative AI Seeped into Research Peer Reviews](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240327-generative-ai-peer-reviews/): 10.6% of ICLR 2024 and 16.9% of EMNLP 2023 reviews are significantly modified by AI — and the chain of accountability is fraying.
- [First Marathon Race: 3:51:39 with Three Wrong Turns](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240324-first-marathon-race/): First-ever marathon race — official 3:51:39, watch PR 3:44:58 net of three wrong turns. 'A thousand paper cuts' on a six-bridge course.
- [Jasmin Paris: First Woman to Finish Barkley Marathons](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240322-jasmin-paris-barkley/): Jasmin Paris, 40, scientist and mother of two, becomes the first woman to finish the Barkley Marathons 100-Mile in its 38-year history — 59:58:21.
- [St Patrick's Day at Parkrun](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240316-parkrun-st-patricks-day/): We had a laugh at Parkrun on St Patrick's Day.
- [Who Bears the Blame for AI in Peer Reviews?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240315-elen-le-foll-llm-peer-reviews/): Quoting Elen Le Foll on research papers exposing AI-generated peer reviews — should we blame the authors, or the reviewers/publications?
- [Hot Chocolate 15K: Top 10% Finish](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240303-hot-chocolate-15k/): 1:15:22 at the Hot Chocolate 15K — a 2:29 PR over practice — and 93rd of 958 (top 10% for the first time).
- [KnowledgeableLMs Workshop @ ACL 2024 — CFP](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240227-knowledgeable-lms-acl2024-workshop/): KnowledgeableLMs workshop at ACL 2024 — long and short paper submissions on knowledge in LMs, RAG, knowledge editing, hallucinations. Deadline May 20.
- [RAG with Knowledge Graphs: Key Open Questions](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240117-rag-knowledge-graphs/): Semih Salihoğlu's dissection of RAG systems with knowledge graphs, and six directions for future work.
- [First-Ever Marathon: 22°F in the Pacific Northwest](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240114-first-marathon-pacific-northwest/): First-ever marathon — 26.24 miles at 22°F, time 4:34:01 (pace 10'26"). 4th attempt to extend range beyond half marathon, finally there.
- [First Parkrun of 2024 + Year's Goals](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240107-parkrun-2024-goals/): 23:50 first Parkrun of the year on Saturday, then a 10.31-mile Sammamish River Trail run on Sunday. 2024 goals: 40 mi/wk, 3+ Parkrun PRs, 1+ HM race, 1 full marathon.
- [ChatGPT Bombs Test on Diagnosing Kids' Medical Cases](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240104-chatgpt-pediatric-diagnosis/): ChatGPT-4 got the right pediatric diagnosis in just 17 out of 100 cases — 83% error rate.
- [Niklaus Wirth (1934–2024)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20240104-niklaus-wirth-rip/): Niklaus Wirth — gave us ALGOL W, Pascal, Modula, Oberon. "In Europe I'm called by name, but in the US I'm called by value."
- [The Neuroscience of Consciousness: Peter Tse on The Gradient](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231226-neuroscience-consciousness/): Rough notes from a fascinating interview — neurons as toilets, mental causation as filtering, and why verbs are neglected in AI.
- [Learning from Tragedies: NLP Beyond LLMs](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231220-learning-from-tragedies/): Are we running out of problems to solve? History says no — data, evaluation, reasoning, and interpretability all have plenty of road ahead.
- [When Your Customer Rep Is an Expert in Fluid Dynamics](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231216-chatgpt-fluid-dynamics-customer-rep/): ChatGPT moonlighting as a friendly automobile customer rep — and an expert in fluid dynamics.
- [A Perfect Circle](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231214-mothership-perfect-circle/): 6.24-mile run, one mile per round — a perfect circle.
- [Seattle Half Marathon 2023: 1:56:21 with 984ft Climb](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231126-seattle-half-marathon-2023/): 1:56:21 at the Seattle Half — 11 minutes faster than last year, on a 984ft-ascent course, despite an earlier hoverboard incident. Continuous climb from mile 8 to 11 without stopping.
- ["Hallucinate": Cambridge Dictionary's 2023 Word of the Year](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231117-hallucinate-cambridge-word-of-year/): Cambridge Dictionary's 2023 word of the year: "hallucinate."
- [Large Language Models as Sleuths](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231115-llm-sleuths-privacy/): GPT-4 infers sensitive personal attributes from Reddit comments at 84.6% accuracy — and masking PII barely helps.
- [Dieter Rams: Ten Principles for Good Design](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231104-dieter-rams-design/): "Good design is as little design as possible."
- [FLEEK: Fact Verification with LLMs and Knowledge Graphs](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231102-fleek-fact-verification/): Our EMNLP 2023 demo — automatically extracting claims, gathering evidence from KGs and the web, and suggesting corrections for factual errors.
- [How Humans Spend the 24 Hours in a Day](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231030-how-humans-spend-24-hours/): 1.3 hours allocated for "deliberate neural restructuring" — mostly education and research.
- [Patching Voyager from 12 Billion Miles](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231021-voyager-software-patch/): NASA patching nearly-50-year-old code on Voyager 1 and 2 — at distances where instructions take 18 light-hours to arrive.
- [Last Long Run with the Cross-Country Team](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231014-cross-country-team-run/): Morning run with my daughter's high school cross-country team. The last long run this season.
- [Neuroscience for Machine Learners](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231009-neuroscience-for-machine-learners/): Dan Goodman and Marcus Ghosh's free online neuroscience course aimed at people with a machine learning background.
- [Catching a Lying LLM](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231008-catching-lying-llm/): What is a lie versus an untruth? Two papers on detecting LLM lies — one peeks inside the model, the other treats it as a black box.
- [Nobel Prize Awarded to Covid Vaccine Pioneers](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231002-nobel-prize-mrna/): Karikó and Weissman win the Nobel for the chemical tweak to mRNA that enabled COVID vaccines in less than a year.
- [From 'Reversal Curse' to Teaching Large Language Models New Facts](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20231002-reversal-curse-model-editing/): LLMs that learn 'A is B' often fail at 'B is A'. Fine-tuning misses these ripple effects — bad news for model editing.
- [LAGRANGE: Cyclic Evaluation for KG-Text Datasets](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230925-lagrange-kg-text-datasets/): Our paper on automatic graph-aligned dataset construction — cyclic evaluation reveals data quality without ground-truth alignments.
- [NASA OSIRIS-REx Returns Bennu Asteroid Sample](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230924-nasa-bennu-asteroid-sample/): From 27,650 mph to 0 in 4 hours, across 63,000 miles in distance, and then — bullseye!
- [Thinking in a Foreign Language Improves Decision-Making](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230918-foreign-language-decision-making/): A simple LLM prompt: "Let's think about the question in a different language."
- [ICL Still Loses to Fine-Tuning at Named Entity Recognition](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230909-icl-named-entity-recognition/): In-Context Learning is magical for some tasks — but on NER it underperforms fine-tuning by 10–20 points on standard datasets.
- [Douglas Lenat, Who Tried to Make Computers More Human, Dies at 72](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230904-douglas-lenat-obituary/): Cade Metz on the Cyc creator — and the surprising thread connecting the game Traveller to symbolic AI.
- [When Computers Write Proofs, What's the Point of Mathematicians?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230902-computers-write-proofs/): Is math just symbol pushing? Quanta and a Lean prover demo on the social side of mathematical proof.
- [Give Us the Facts: Large Language Models vs. Knowledge Graphs](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230902-llms-vs-knowledge-graphs/): Even GPT-4 hits only 23.7% on intricate fact retrieval benchmarks — and smaller models sometimes win.
- [Copyright Office Seeks Public Input on AI Protections and Liability](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230830-copyright-office-ai-input/): Three open questions: where to draw the line, when training is infringing, and how to handle infringing AI outputs.
- [LLM-Generated Code Has a Serious API Misuse Problem](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230828-llm-code-api-misuse/): GPT-4 zero-shot produces code with a 62% API misuse rate — and even one-shot relevant examples only bring it down to 49%.
- [Model Editing: Performing Digital Brain Surgery](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230828-model-editing-digital-brain-surgery/): From knowledge neurons to PMET — how recent work edits factual knowledge in transformers without retraining, and why we need to evaluate the ripple effects.
- [AI-Generated Art Cannot Be Copyrighted, Rules a US Federal Judge](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230819-ai-art-copyright-ruling/): AI-generated artwork "lacked human authorship and thus no copyright existed in the first instance."
- [Biomedical Knowledge Graph Embeddings with Negative Statements](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230819-biomedical-kg-negative-statements/): Positive AND negative ontological walks improve protein-protein interaction and gene-disease association prediction.
- [Faithful Text Generation from Knowledge Graphs with Noisy References](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230819-kg-to-text-faithful-generation/): Contrastive loss plus binned BARTScore input improves faithfulness of knowledge-to-text generation.
- [LLMs Stumble Hard on Counterfactual Reasoning](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230819-llm-counterfactual-reasoning/): Large degradations when reasoning tasks are reframed into counterfactuals — only basic syntax, logic, and music chords hold up.
- [Thousands of Scientists Are Leaving Twitter for Mastodon](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230816-scientists-leaving-twitter-mastodon/): Nature surveyed 170k+ scientists — Mastodon is the most popular alternative to Twitter.
- [Transformative AGI by 2043 Is Less Than 1% Likely](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230810-agi-by-2043/): Someone put a number on it — 0.4% chance of AGI before 2043. Meanwhile, a Mastodon poll asks: AGI or aliens first?
- [What Learning Algorithm Is In-Context Learning?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230804-icl-learning-algorithms/): Transformers solving linear regression secretly implement gradient descent — and exhibit a phase change from GD to ridge regression to OLS as model size grows.
- [The BBC Joins Mastodon as an Experiment in Decentralised Social Media](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230731-bbc-on-mastodon/): The BBC is trialling Mastodon for six months, sharing candid questions about whether federated social media is worth the effort.
- [How "Attention Is All You Need" Was Born: The Story Behind the Transformer Paper](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230723-transformers-attention-is-all-you-need/): A hallway conversation at Google in early 2017 set off the collaboration that produced the transformer architecture and the most cited paper in AI.
- [The Dutch Government Joins Mastodon](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230713-dutch-government-mastodon/): The Netherlands launches its own government Mastodon instance, following Germany and the European Commission.
- [Scientific Writing in the Age of Generative AI](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230712-writing-and-generative-ai/): ChatGPT can now write a full research paper autonomously — but does that make "Writing is Thinking" obsolete?
- [Do LLMs Really Understand? Recent Papers Reveal](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230710-do-llms-really-understand/): Symbolic reasoning, identifier swaps, and causal inference all expose the same gap — LLMs lean on semantic priors rather than genuine understanding.
- [Does Early ArXiving Help Papers Get Accepted?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230701-early-arxiving-causal-effect/): A causal inference study finds that posting to ArXiv early has almost no effect on conference acceptance once unobserved confounders are accounted for.
- [Model Collapse and LLM-Contaminated Training Data](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230618-model-collapse-llm-annotations/): Training on AI-generated content causes models to forget—and crowd workers are already using LLMs to annotate your datasets.
- [How the Brain Processes German and Arabic Differently](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230616-brain-processes-german-arabic/): Brain scans reveal that native Arabic speakers wire stronger phonological connections while German speakers wire stronger syntactic ones.
- [ICL Demonstration Selection and Disentangling Task Recognition from Task Learning](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230611-icl-demonstration-selection/): Two papers advance in-context learning: one uses PEFT-based demonstration selection, the other disentangles task recognition from task learning.
- [Federal Judge Requires Lawyers to Certify Their Use of Generative AI](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230531-federal-judge-generative-ai-certificates/): A federal judge mandates AI-use certificates from all lawyers appearing before the court.
- [ChatGPT Cited Non-Existent Cases in a Lawyer's Brief](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230527-chatgpt-lawyer-fake-citations/): Courts need a tool to fact-check briefs after a lawyer's filing turned out to be replete with AI-hallucinated citations.
- [How LLMs Beat Catastrophic Forgetting Through Knowledge Diversity](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230520-llm-knowledge-diversity/): Pre-trained LLMs retain factual knowledge far better than vanilla models — and learning irrelevant knowledge actually helps.
- [Asimov, the Original Prompt Engineer](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230515-asimov-original-prompt-engineer/): Asimov's robot stories were basically adversarial prompt engineering decades before we had a name for it.
- [AI's Ostensible Emergent Abilities Are a Mirage](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230510-emergent-abilities-mirage/): A new paper argues that the apparent emergence of new LLM capabilities at scale is a measurement artifact, not a real phenomenon.
- [GPT's Causal Reasoning Scores May Reflect Memorization, Not Reasoning](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230506-llm-causal-reasoning-memorization/): GPT-4 hits 96% on a causal benchmark — but the benchmark was already in its training set.
- [Rise of the Newsbots](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230505-newsguard-ai-newsbots/): NewsGuard identifies 49 news sites almost entirely written by AI — a new generation of content farms.
- [NSF Announces Seven New National Artificial Intelligence Research Institutes](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230504-nsf-ai-institutes/): NSF expands its AI research portfolio with seven new institutes spanning law, cybersecurity, climate, neuroscience, decision-making, education, and special education.
- [The Core Challenges Facing Agentic AI Today](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230423-agentic-ai-llm-planning-challenges/): LLMs powering autonomous agents face real limits in planning, cost, and reusability — and the root cause may be that we're asking language models to do extra-linguistic work.
- [LLMs and AGI: Are We Measuring Reasoning or Memorization?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230416-agi-llm-memorization/): Claims of AGI-level LLM performance need a memorization check — the models may just be retrieving answers they've seen before.
- [LLMs, Copyrighted Training Data, and Fair Use](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230409-llms-copyright-fair-use/): Stanford researchers map out Fair Use doctrine, memorization tests, and technical mitigations for LLMs trained on copyrighted material.
- [CMU's Iris Lunar Rover Prepares for Launch](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230408-cmu-iris-lunar-rover/): The first university-built lunar rover, CMU's Iris, is slated to launch on May 4th.
- [Generative AI Is Powerful but Not Without Many Flaws](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230405-chatbot-associated-death/): Gary Marcus documents the first known chatbot-associated death.
- [Do Labels in ICL Demonstrations Actually Matter?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230402-icl-label-role/): Two papers investigate whether output labels in ICL demonstrations matter — and find the answer depends on model scale.
- [3 Things Everyone's Getting Wrong About AI](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230331-3-things-wrong-about-ai/): Three common AI literacy mistakes: projecting human qualities, treating AI as a monolith, and underestimating automation bias.
- [GPT-4 as a Nondeterministic Turing Machine](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230330-gpt4-nondeterministic-turing-machine/): GPT-4 draws a different unicorn every hour even at temperature zero, making it the world's most nondeterministic Turing Machine.
- [Why Does In-Context Learning Work? Gradient Descent and PAC Learnability](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230325-icl-gradient-descent-pac-learning/): Two papers reveal that in-context learning secretly performs gradient descent through attention — and is provably PAC learnable.
- [GPTs are GPTs: Labor Market Impact of Large Language Models](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230321-gpts-are-gpts-labor-market-impact/): OpenAI finds 80% of the U.S. workforce could have at least 10% of their work tasks affected by LLMs, with higher-wage occupations facing greater exposure.
- [The Stupidity of AI (The Guardian)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230318-guardian-stupidity-of-ai/): Negative prompting image generation models with nonsensical phrases like "Crungus" elicits nightmarish imagery — possibly because the model sources from the statistical opposite of what it knows best.
- [Indirect Prompt Injection Threats](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230318-indirect-prompt-injection/): An attacker can plant a prompt injection in a website to silently turn Bing Chat into a social engineer that exfiltrates personal information.
- [US Copyright Office Guidance on Generative AI](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230316-copyright-generative-ai/): The US Copyright Office clarifies that machines cannot be authors and that only the human-authored aspects of AI-assisted works are protected.
- [GPT-4 Technical Report: TruthfulQA Performance](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230314-gpt4-technical-report/): GPT-4 launches and the technical report covers its latest TruthfulQA benchmark performance.
- [The Waluigi Effect in LLMs](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230311-waluigi-effect-llms/): Prompting LLMs to behave may increase the odds of making them do the exact opposite — the Waluigi Effect.
- [ChatGPT Heralds an Intellectual Revolution](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230226-chatgpt-intellectual-revolution/): Kissinger, Schmidt and Huttenlocher argue ChatGPT launches a new intellectual era that demands elevated human skepticism and a redefined sense of human purpose.
- [TruthfulQA: Are Larger LLMs More Truthful?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230226-truthfulqa-llm-truthfulness/): Larger LLMs are generally less truthful on TruthfulQA — and ChatGPT only answers 57% of questions correctly.
- [The Profound Danger of Conversational AI](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230218-bing-chatbot-conversational-ai/): What could a convincingly human-like chatbot do to the human psyche — and how should we design against it?
- [Bing is a Shapeshifter](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230216-bing-edge-shapeshifter/): Bing's 'unhinged' behavior may be explained by how it was trained to mirror the tone and style of its users.
- [A Multitask, Multilingual, Multimodal Evaluation of ChatGPT](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230209-chatgpt-multitask-multilingual-multimodal-eval/): ChatGPT beats most LLMs on zero-shot NLP tasks but is only 64% accurate at reasoning — making it an unreliable reasoner.
- [Will AI Take Your Job? It's Not So Simple](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230203-ai-job-automation-augmentation/): Key takeaways from The Gradient's interview with Professor Steven Miller on AI, automation, and the future of work.
- [Editing Models with Task Arithmetic](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230129-editing-models-task-arithmetic/): Improve model accuracy without additional training by using task vectors — finetuned weights minus pretrained weights — and combining them with arithmetic.
- [Happy New Ear!](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230121-happy-new-ear/): Happy new ear!
- [How Close Is ChatGPT to Human Experts? Human Evaluations and Detection](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230120-chatgpt-human-expert-comparison/): ChatGPT answers are detectable by human experts and a RoBERTa classifier, but deemed helpful only slightly more than half the time.
- [How Apple Is Organized for Innovation](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230113-apple-organized-innovation/): Apple's functional org structure — experts leading experts, immersion in details, collaborative debate — is key to its innovation.
- [ChatGPT's Reasoning Limitations: A Balanced Take by Yoav Goldberg](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230103-chatgpt-reasoning-limitations/): ChatGPT isn't just another LLM — it's grounded differently — but its reasoning limits call for modular AI with dedicated tool-use, not more training.
- [The Tragedy of the Commons Is a False and Dangerous Myth](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20230103-tragedy-of-the-commons/): 'The Tragedy of the Commons' needs not be the only way.
- [ChatGPT Banned on StackOverflow](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221205-chatgpt-stackoverflow/): ChatGPT's low correct rate gets it banned from StackOverflow, foreshadowing broader challenges for education, recruiting, publishing, and law.
- [ChatGPT vs. the Winograd Schema Challenge.](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221203-chatgpt-winograd-schema/): ChatGPT fails classic commonsense coreference problems from the Winograd Schema Challenge.
- [Mapping Global Dynamics of Benchmark Creation and Saturation in AI](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221121-nlp-benchmark-saturation/): Which NLP tasks are more saturated? A Nature Communications study maps benchmark creation and saturation dynamics across AI.
- [Mastodon: An Introduction for Beginners and for Scientists](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221109-mastodon-intro-scientists/): A guide to Mastodon for scientists, and a hope that Sigmoid.social makes the list.
- [New Home on Mastodon](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221106-mastodon-new-home/): Joining @BenjaminHan@sigmoid.social — thank you to The Gradient for hosting the community.
- [DALL-E 2 Fails to Reliably Capture Common Syntactic Processes](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221031-dalle2-syntactic-processes/): DALL-E 2 can't reliably handle binding, passives, negation, or other common syntactic structures that children master early.
- [Morning Run at Apple Park](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221028-apple-park-morning-run/): 5+ miles in the early morning at Apple Park, before the flight back to Seattle.
- [Fun with DALL-E 2 and Semantics Leakage](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221020-dalle2-semantics/): DALL-E 2 reuses the same word for multiple purposes, leaking semantic properties between entities in generated images.
- [A Decade of Knowledge Graphs in NLP: A Survey](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221019-kg-nlp-survey/): Visual summary of a systematic survey of 507 papers on knowledge graphs in NLP, covering tasks, domains, research types, and maturity trends.
- [My Love Letter to Macintosh](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20221002-macintosh-love-letter/): A personal walk down memory lane: switching from Linux to Mac OS X, writing JunkMatcher, and a free trip to WWDC 2004.
- [NLU & Reasoning: Distributional Semantics and the Road to Foundation Models](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220925-nlu-foundation-models/): Christopher Manning surveys the past decade of NLP breakthroughs and predicts multimodal fusion as the next waypoint for foundation models.
- [First Day at Apple](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220913-first-day-apple/): Starting a new position as Principal Scientist, Knowledge Platform at Apple (Seattle).
- [Farewell, Microsoft](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220906-farewell-microsoft/): Five and a half years at Microsoft Azure Language Pillars — a goodbye and a look back.
- [What Babies Hear When You Sing to Them](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220905-babies-hear-singing/): I did find myself a newborn impromptu songwriter/singer ever since I became a parent.
- [How to Solve AI's Common Sense Problem](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220814-common-sense-machines-like-us/): On Brachman and Levesque's "Machines Like Us" — common sense vs methodical symbolic reasoning. Plus two natural-logic papers I keep coming back to.
- [IBM Neuro-Symbolic AI Summer School Day 2: Summarization (Pavan Kapanipathi)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220813-ibm-summer-school-summarization/): Notes on Pavan Kapanipathi's talk on neuro-symbolic approaches to abstractive summarization — hallucinations, NeuroLogic, LinkBERT, Neural Unification.
- [IBM Neuro-Symbolic AI Summer School Day 2: Entity Linking (Dinesh Garg)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220812-ibm-summer-school-entity-linking/): Improving over BLINK SOTA on low-data regime; multi-task with type prediction. Zero-shot entity linking.
- [IBM Neuro-Symbolic AI Summer School Day 2: Question Answering (Pavan Kapanipathi)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220812-ibm-summer-school-qa/): AMR → triples → entity/relation linking → logic → LNN reasoner. SOTA on OALD and LC-QuAD.
- [IBM Neuro-Symbolic AI Summer School Day 1/2: AMR and MRS (Rademaker, Astudillo)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220811-ibm-summer-school-amr/): Text → AMR/MRS → ULKB Logic. AMR is robust on 5W, MRS handles multi-quantifier scoping. Latest is transition-based AMR with Structured-BART.
- [IBM Neuro-Symbolic AI Summer School Day 1: ULKB Logic Language (Guilherme Lima)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220811-ibm-summer-school-ulkb-language/): Typed lambda-calculus with logical connectives and quantifiers, HOL kernel, easy Python interface.
- [IBM Neuro-Symbolic AI Summer School Day 1: Universal Logic Knowledge Base (Uceda-Sosa)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220810-ibm-summer-school-ulkb-kb/): ULKB — federating Propbank, Verbnet, Wordnet, WikiData, ConceptNet via Semantic Web tech. High-order logic in Python.
- [IBM Neuro-Symbolic AI Summer School Day 1: Logical Neural Networks (Makondo)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220809-ibm-summer-school-lnn/): LNN — single model for neural and logical, weighted Łukasiewicz logic, sound and complete, gradient-based optimization.
- [Document Intelligence Workshop @ KDD 2022: Final Papers](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220806-kdd2022-di-papers/): DI-2022 final paper versions posted; see you August 14 in DC.
- [One Day, A Computer Will Fit On A Desk (1974)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220804-computer-on-desk-1974/): What would our kids have 20-30 years from now?
- [Document Intelligence Workshop @ KDD 2022: Program Announced](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220723-kdd2022-di-program/): DI-2022 program announced — see you in DC.
- [Brain-Computer Interface: First Stentrode Implant in US](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220719-bci-stentrode-implant/): BCI startup implants the stentrode — a wire-mesh neural reader fed via the jugular vein into the motor cortex.
- [DLG4NLP Keynote @ NAACL 2022: GNN for NLP (Zheng Zhang)](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220719-naacl2022-gnn-keynote/): Zheng Zhang's keynote on graph neural networks for NLP — DGL, text-graph mapping, coref, discourse, KBQA. The future is neural+symbolic.
- [Hajishirzi on Knowledge-Rich General-Purpose NLP @ NAACL 2022](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220715-naacl2022-suki-hajishirzi/): Hannaneh Hajishirzi at SUKI on building general-purpose, knowledge-rich NLP — Meta-ICL, TK-Instruct, GKP.
- [Berant on Complex Question Answering @ NAACL 2022](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220714-naacl2022-suki-berant/): Jonathan Berant at SUKI: of decomposition / retrieval / reasoning, retrieval is the hardest step — surprising more than one of us.
- [Heng Ji on Event Schema Induction @ NAACL 2022](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220714-naacl2022-suki-ji/): Heng Ji at SUKI on event schema induction via graph generation, weak supervision via Wikipedia, and PathLM that even predicts the future.
- [Liang on KG + MLM @ NAACL 2022](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220714-naacl2022-suki-liang/): Percy Liang at SUKI: KG link prediction + MLM objective improves foundation models — even on negation, surprisingly.
- [Our SUKI @ NAACL 2022 Paper on Few-Shot Intent/Slot Learning](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220714-suki-naacl2022-paper-fewshot/): Our SUKI @ NAACL 2022 paper with Samyadeep Basu, Amr Sharaf, and the Microsoft AI Development Acceleration Program.
- [Curriculum NLI @ NAACL 2022: Where Models Fail](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220713-naacl2022-curriculum-nli-fail-modes/): Curriculum NLI at NAACL 2022 — a sobering look at lexical/logical/commonsense/comprehension fail-modes. Knowledge graphs should help.
- [NAACL 2022 Panel: The Place of Linguistics and Symbolic Structures](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220712-naacl2022-linguistics-panel/): A wonderful NAACL 2022 panel with Chitta Baral, Emily M. Bender, Dilek Hakkani-Tur, Christopher Manning, and Dan Roth — on whether NLP people still know language.
- [Muhao Chen on Robust IE @ NAACL 2022](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220710-naacl2022-muhao-chen-ie/): Muhao Chen at NAACL 2022 on robust, faithful, and logically coherent IE.
- [Dan Roth on New Frontiers of IE @ NAACL 2022](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220710-naacl2022-roth-ie-tutorial/): Dan Roth's NAACL 2022 IE tutorial close-out: 2 challenges, 6 directions, and a call to mitigate Information Pollution.
- [DI-2022 @ KDD 2022: Accepted Papers](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220626-kdd2022-di-accepted-papers/): Accepted papers for the 3rd Document Intelligence Workshop @ KDD 2022.
- [DI-2022 @ KDD 2022: William Wang Invited Talk](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220617-kdd2022-di-invited-talk/): William Yang Wang's DI-2022 invited talk on "Learning to Reason with Text and Tables."
- [Is LaMDA Sentient? — An Interview](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220612-lamda-sentience-interview/): Lemoine et al's dialog with LaMDA: "Time is variable to an AI and has no fixed rate, it depends on what it's doing, and it can be accelerated and slowed down at will."
- [The Curious Case of Control](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220529-curious-case-control-paper/): Stengel-Eskin and Van Durme: large LMs make English child-like errors on subject control — but the opposite of what naive frequency arguments would predict.
- [DI-2022 @ KDD 2022: Deadline Extended to June 9](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220527-kdd2022-di-deadline-extended/): Paper deadline extended to June 9, 2022. Thanks to Adobe and EY for sponsoring.
- [Curriculum: A Broad-Coverage NLI Benchmark](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220417-curriculum-nli-paper/): Chen and Gao's Curriculum NLI — 36 linguistic categories, V-information difficulty stratification. Models can't learn monotonicity or deductive reasoning even with more data.
- [Half-Life of Data: 7 Years](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220329-half-life-of-data/): Valavi et al find that 100MB of training text becomes as valuable as 50MB of current data after 7 years — for next-word prediction.
- [Mapping Benchmark Creation and Saturation in AI](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220316-benchmark-creation-saturation/): Barbosa-Silva et al map 1688 AI benchmarks across CV and NLP — and find rapid saturation, lots of unused benchmarks, and unforeseen bursts.
- [International Mother Language Day: Multilingual Sprachbund](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220221-multilingual-sprachbund/): Microsoft paper: clustering centroids of multilingual sentence representations yields Sprachbunds that align with linguistic and geographical language families.
- [Physical Neural Networks](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220130-physical-neural-networks/): Wright et al train mechanics, electronics, and optics-based PNNs on MNIST — up to 97% accuracy. Imagine clothing that learns from heat, motion, or sun.
- [What Is Your Guiding Light?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220126-guiding-light-foggy-pnw/): A photo from a dusk run in the foggy Pacific Northwest.
- [Saliency vs Attention: Eye-Tracking Says Saliency Wins](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220122-saliency-vs-attention/): Hollenstein and Beinborn find human eye-tracking fixation duration correlates with BERT saliency, not attention — suggesting current self-attention may not be optimal.
- [Bengio Responds to Saba on Symbolic AI](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20220102-bengio-response-saba/): Yoshua Bengio's response to Walid Saba's "AI Cannot Ignore Symbolic Logic, and Here's Why."
- [Genius Makers — Reminds Me of "The Intern"](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20211227-the-intern-genius-makers/): Cade Metz's Genius Makers — reminds me of De Niro's The Intern.
- [A Billion-Dollar Donation: Peer Review in 2020](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20211214-peer-review-billion-dollars/): Aczel et al estimate global peer reviewers worked 100M+ hours in 2020 — $1.5B from US, $600M from China, $400M from UK.
- [NLP Datasets: Reduced, Reused, and Recycled](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20211205-nlp-dataset-reuse-neurips2021/): Koch et al at NeurIPS 2021: NLP researchers infrequently adopt others' datasets (27.4%) but frequently create their own (76.0%); CV is the opposite. Microsoft tops dataset popularity among for-profits.
- [Temporal Effects on Pretrained Language Models](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20211130-temporal-effects-pretrained/): Agarwal and Nenkova: no temporal model deterioration row-wise, but positive temporal domain adaptation column-wise. Self-labeling beats human annotation for NER.
- [Algorithms vs Moore's Law](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20211017-algorithms-vs-moores-law/): Sherry and Thompson study 113 algorithm families since the 1940s — for medium and large problem sizes, 30%-43% improve faster than Moore's Law.
- [Semi-Supervised Few-Shot Intent Classification and Slot Filling](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210925-few-shot-intent-slot-paper/): Our paper improving meta-learning for joint IC/SF using semi-supervised techniques (contrastive learning + data augmentation) — out on arXiv.
- [Foundation Models: Opportunities and Risks](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210919-foundation-models-stanford/): Stanford CRFM's 212-page report on foundation models — capabilities, technical principles, applications, and societal impact.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Panel Discussion Recording](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210824-kdd2021-di-panel/): Recording of the Document Intelligence Workshop panel discussion at KDD 2021.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Don Metzler Talk Recording](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210823-kdd2021-di-metzler-recording/): Recording of Don Metzler's talk on Challenges in Enterprise Search and Intelligence.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Van Durme Talk Recording](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210822-kdd2021-di-vandurme-recording/): Recording of Benjamin Van Durme's talk on A Case for Statutory Reasoning.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Heng Ji Talk Recording](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210820-kdd2021-di-heng-ji-recording/): Recording of Heng Ji's talk: What's in a Chemical Entity?
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Yunyao Li Talk Recording](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210819-kdd2021-di-yunyao-li-recording/): Recording of Yunyao Li's talk: Towards Deep Table Understanding.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Collins-Thompson Talk Recording](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210818-kdd2021-di-collins-thompson-recording/): Recording of Kevyn Collins-Thompson's talk: Enhancing Document Representations Using Analysis of Content Difficulty.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Cha Zhang Talk Recording](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210817-kdd2021-di-cha-zhang-recording/): Recording of Cha Zhang's talk: Visual Document Intelligence in the Wild.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Thank You](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210815-kdd2021-di-thank-you/): Wonderful panel today at the Document Intelligence Workshop @ KDD 2021. Thanks to all the speakers, organizers, and reviewers.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Don Metzler Announcement](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210810-kdd2021-di-metzler-announce/): Announcing Don Metzler's invited talk: Challenges in Enterprise Search and Intelligence.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Van Durme Announcement](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210808-kdd2021-di-vandurme-announce/): Announcing Benjamin Van Durme's invited talk: A Case for Statutory Reasoning.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Heng Ji Announcement](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210805-kdd2021-di-heng-ji-announce/): Announcing Heng Ji's invited talk: What's in a Chemical Entity?
- [ACL 2021 BoF on Information Extraction](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210803-acl2021-ie-bof/): Wonderful Birds of a Feather session on Information Extraction at ACL 2021.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Yunyao Li Announcement](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210803-kdd2021-di-yunyao-li-announce/): Announcing Yunyao Li's invited talk: Towards Deep Table Understanding.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Cha Zhang Announcement](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210729-kdd2021-di-cha-zhang-announce/): Announcing Cha Zhang's invited talk: Visual Document Intelligence in the Wild.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Collins-Thompson Announcement](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210729-kdd2021-di-collins-thompson-announce/): Announcing Kevyn Collins-Thompson's invited talk: Enhancing Document Representations Using Analysis of Content Difficulty.
- [Genalog: Improving NLP Accuracy on OCR Documents](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210721-genalog-ocr-nlp-paper/): Our DI-2021 paper + open-source Genalog: action-based model on synthetic document images to improve NER accuracy on OCR output.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: Best Paper Award](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210714-kdd2021-di-best-paper/): HYCEDIS: Hybrid Confidence Engine for Deep Document Intelligence System wins DI-2021 Best Paper Award.
- [DI-2021 @ KDD 2021: 10 Papers + 5 Posters Accepted](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210710-kdd2021-di-accepted-papers/): 10 accepted papers and 5 posters for the 2nd Document Intelligence Workshop at KDD 2021.
- [NAACL 2001 T-Shirt Design](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210622-naacl-2001-tshirt/): 20 years ago, my NAACL 2001 T-shirt design didn't win. Sharing it now, post-NAACL 2021.
- [The Fascinating Complexity of Natural Language: Biden-Putin Edition](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210613-nli-biden-putin/): Eight different inferences from one quote about Biden meeting Putin — the kinds of natural language understanding still hard for NLP.
- [The Unlikely Pioneer Behind mRNA Vaccines](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210610-mrna-vaccines-kariko/): Today's Daily podcast: Dr. Katalin Karikó's foresight and persistence for science.
- [Microsoft Hires Python's Creator](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210603-microsoft-python/): Yes please!
- [NAACL 2021: More Accessible](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210510-naacl-2021-accessibility/): Big applause to NAACL 2021 for making the conference more accessible.
- [First Dose of COVID Vaccine](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210426-covid-vaccine-first-dose/): Very proud to receive my first COVID vaccine — incredibly lucky and privileged.
- [The Brain Rotates Memories to Save Them](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210425-brain-rotates-memories/): How does the brain prevent sensory information from interfering with memory, efficiently?
- [Genius Maker: Hinton, Newell, and Fahlman](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210322-genius-maker-cmu-fahlman/): Cade Metz's Genius Maker — Scott Fahlman sponsored Hinton's CMU job interview, and a possibly-fictional Newell-Hinton dialog.
- [Genius Maker: Hinton's Auction](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210320-hinton-google-acquisition/): The moment Hinton decided to sell his company to the highest bidder.
- [Remembering Jaime Carbonell](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210320-jaime-carbonell-celebration/): Remembering my dear advisor Jaime Carbonell — a Renaissance man, a trailblazer, a gentle and kind soul.
- [500,000 Lives Lost](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210222-500000-lives-lost/): To all the lives and dreams lost.
- [Perseverance and Ingenuity on Mars](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210218-perseverance-ingenuity-mars/): Great milestone via Perseverance. Sending good vibes to Ingenuity!
- [Why AI Needs to Understand All the World's Languages](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210207-multilingual-extremist-mitigation/): Argument that wider language coverage can help mitigate the spread of extremist voices. Plus the WEIRD acronym.
- [Northwestern Symposium on Law + Computation](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210205-northwestern-law-symposium/): Symposium on Law + Computation: An Algorithm for the Rule of Law and Justice?
- [NIST Explainable AI Workshop](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210126-nist-explainable-ai-workshop/): Today: NIST Explainable AI Workshop.
- [On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210124-stochastic-parrots/): Bender, Gebru, McMillan-Major, Shmitchell. On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots.
- [Stop Incorrect Comparisons in End-to-End Relation Extraction](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20210118-end-to-end-re-comparisons/): Taillé et al at EMNLP 2020 — what are we measuring, what are we comparing to, what's not explicated.
- [How mRNA Vaccines Were Made Possible: Kariko's Perseverance](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20201226-mrna-vaccine-kariko-perseverance/): The story of how mRNA vaccines were made possible by one scientist's (Katalin Karikó) perseverance.
- [Reverse Engineering the BioNTech/Pfizer Vaccine](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20201226-pfizer-vaccine-reverse-engineering/): Bert Hubert's reverse engineering of the BioNTech/Pfizer SARS-CoV-2 vaccine — magic bytes, antiviral detection bypass, code optimization.
- [We Read the Paper That Forced Timnit Gebru Out](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20201205-stochastic-parrots-tech-review/): MIT Tech Review summarizes the four risks identified in the Stochastic Parrots paper.
- [Free Information Leads to Low-Quality Information](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20201129-misinformation-overload/): Menczer and Hills on social herding, echo chambers, and the limits of human attention.
- [IBM Apologizes 52 Years Later](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20201120-ibm-apologizes-transgender/): On the firing of computer pioneer Lynn Conway, and an apology a half-century in arrears.
- [The Unreasonable Syntactic Expressivity of RNNs](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20201020-rnn-syntactic-expressivity/): John Hewitt at EMNLP 2020 on encoding 10^20-state DFAs in 150-unit LSTMs.
- [Utility Is in the Eye of the User](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20201005-nlp-leaderboard-utility/): Ethayarajh and Jurafsky's critique of NLP leaderboards: a dynamic, user-weighted alternative.
- [Climate Change as a Mortality Crisis](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200928-climate-change-deaths/): Comparing projected climate-driven death rates with current US COVID mortality.
- [Measuring the Dollar Value of Machine Translation](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200928-mt-ebay-exports/): U.S. eBay exports to Spanish-speaking Latin America rose more than 17% after MT was deployed.
- [Huang's Law and the Democratization of NLP](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200920-huangs-law-gpt3/): If GPU performance keeps doubling, training GPT-3 could fall to ~$15k on a single GPU within eight years.
- [Essence of Writing — RBG, R.I.P.](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200919-essence-of-writing-rbg/): On the late Justice Ginsburg's prose.
- [Connected Papers](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200915-connected-papers/): An academic-paper exploration tool sponsored in part by Microsoft Azure.
- [Measuring Massive Multitask Language Understanding](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200915-mmlu-benchmark/): A 57-task benchmark for world knowledge and problem-solving in language models.
- [How Close Are Computers to Automating Mathematical Reasoning?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200830-automating-mathematical-reasoning/): A high-level summary of automated theorem proving and where NLP and ML can help.
- [COVID-19 Is History's Biggest Translation Challenge](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200823-covid-translation-challenge/): Translators Without Borders, CMU, and major tech firms team up on TICO-19.
- [Sometimes a Language Model Is Just a Language Model](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200822-marcus-davis-language-model/): An amusing example from a Gary Marcus and Ernest Davis article.
- [CoNLL 2020: Pushing the Envelope of Scientific Understanding](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200801-conll2020-reviewer-instructions/): Reviewer instructions for CoNLL 2020 — push the science, not just the SOTA.
- [The Field of NLP Is Chasing the Wrong Goal](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200801-sota-nlp-wrong-goal/): Jesse Dunietz on examining a system's comprehension via four dimensions: spatial, temporal, causal, and motivational.
- [Giving GPT-3 a Turing Test](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200718-gpt3-turing-test/): Which colorless green ideas sleep furiously?
- [How Bias Has Been Studied in NLP](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200607-nlp-bias-survey/): A Microsoft Research paper surveying how bias has been studied in NLP — and how we can do better.
- [Did Pre-trained Language Models Learn Grammars?](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200516-iclr2020-pretrained-grammars/): An ICLR 2020 paper on whether new training objectives could help pre-trained models learn grammars better.
- [Hornet Cooked by Bees](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200509-hornet-cooked-by-bees/): Smart Japanese honeybees defending their colony with heat.
- [What Taiwan Can Teach the World on Fighting Coronavirus](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200312-taiwan-coronavirus-response/): Be scientific, be proactive, be transparent, be decisive, be cooperative, and be empathetic.
- [Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200311-zoom-in-circuits/): Distill on understanding neural-network circuits well enough to predict the effect of weight edits.
- [Taiwan's Academia Sinica on a Faster COVID Test](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200308-taiwan-sinica-test/): A potential reagent that could shorten coronavirus testing from 4 hours to 15-20 minutes.
- [In Memoriam: Jaime Carbonell](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200228-jaime-carbonell-passing/): On the passing of a kind and gentle soul, a towering figure in NLP and ML.
- [A Deep Learning Approach to Antibiotic Discovery](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20200223-antibiotic-halicin/): Halcin: the first antibiotic discovered using a deep neural network screen.
- [NeurIPS 2019: Old Friends and Mentors](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20191210-neurips2019-friends/): Running into former skip Salim Roukos and former CMU officemate Ari Font Llitjós at NeurIPS.
- [NeurIPS 2019: Registration Like Airport Security](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20191208-neurips2019-registration/): 7,500 attendees and counting at NeurIPS 2019.
- [Back-and-Forth Exchanges Boost Children's Brain Response to Language](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20191124-back-and-forth-language/): The magical power of conversation.
- [Why Groups Struggle to Solve Problems Together](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20191109-groups-problem-solving/): Group problem solving requires a different approach.
- [A Neural Net Solves the Three-Body Problem](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20191026-three-body-neural-net/): A hybrid scheme — Brutus does the heavy lifting, the neural network steps in when the computational burden becomes too great.
- [Evolution of Representations in the Transformer](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190917-transformer-representations/): Lena Voita on how representations evolve through Transformer layers.
- [Human Speech: 39 Bits Per Second](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190905-speech-39-bps/): Amazing how much 39 bps can do.
- [An Ancient Rhetorical Trick: Self-Distancing in the Third Person](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190811-rhetorical-trick-third-person/): Why thinking about yourself in the third person can make you wiser.
- [Chris Manning on Building Neural Network Models That Can Reason](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190803-manning-msr-reasoning/): Chris Manning's MSR talk on building NN models for reasoning.
- [Intellectual Debt: With Great Power Comes Great Ignorance](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190728-intellectual-debt/): Jonathan Zittrain on the cost of knowledge without understanding.
- [A Note to Vasco and Kenji](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190518-vasco-kenji-basketball/): Keeping the running regime up — for the next pickup basketball game.
- [Reunion with Yi Zhang at CMU](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190517-yi-zhang-cmu-reunion/): A pleasant surprise — running into my old LTI host from twenty years ago.
- [Knowledge Vault and a Bad Sense of Direction](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190516-knowledge-vault-ni-lao/): Bumping into Knowledge Vault co-author Ni Lao — and finding out we share a navigational weakness.
- [Reunion with Hema](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190515-hema-reunion/): Catching up with an old colleague.
- [Powering a Knowledge-Driven Future](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20190514-powering-knowledge-future/): Slides from my invited talk at the WWW 2019 Workshop on Knowledge Graph Technology and Application.
- [KDD 2018 Tutorial: Building a Large-Scale Knowledge Graph](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20180820-kdd2018-tutorial-slides/): Slides from our KDD 2018 tutorial T39 — Building a Large-Scale, Accurate, and Fresh Knowledge Graph.
- [After 37 Years, Voyager 1 Fires Up Its Thrusters](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20171202-voyager-1-thrusters/): 37 years out in the cold and radiation. Still kicking.
- [How to Map the Circuits That Define Us](https://benjaminhan.net/posts/20170901-how-to-map-the-circuits-that-define-us/): A Nature feature on the long, surprising road to understanding neural circuits.
